revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Iwafune Kofun]][[ja:飯梨岩舟古墳]]
{{Short description|Ancient tomb in Yasugi, Shimane, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Iwafune Kofun | native_name =岩舟古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Iinashi Iwafune Kofun, gaikan-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Iwafune Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Iwafune Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.399917|N|133.190111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yasugi|qid=Q851114|1=Yasugi, Shimane}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|24|00|N|133|11|25|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Iwafune Kofun'''|岩舟古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Iwafune neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yasugi, Shimane|en|Yasugi, Shimane|qid=Q851114|lt=Yasugi, Shimane|1=Yasugi, Shimane}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1948.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202506|title=岩舟古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 10, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Iwafune Kofun is located on the left bank of the Iiri River, halfway up a hill. The tumulus itself is largely lost, so the original shape and type of ''kofun'' is unknown, and only the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remains. This burial chamber is made from cut {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} stones, and is about 2 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.7 meters high. It is believed that the burial chamber once had an antechamber, as indicated by a groove into which the side wall stones of an antechamber would have fitted, but this portion has also disappeared except for two flooring stones. The ceiling of the burial chamber is house-shaped, the side walls are made of a single monolithic stones, and the back wall is made of two stones. Inside are stone fragments from a demolished house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}}. The tumulus is thought to date from the 6th century. It is located approximately five kilometers southwest of {{ill|Arashima Station|en|Arashima Station|qid=Q4784373|lt=Arashima Station|1=Arashima Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20967 Shimane Prefecture Tourism Federation] {{in lang|ja}}
